weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, few clouds. Sunday: 11°C, scattered clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend looks perfect for exploring! I've found some fantastic options for your 3-year-old in Hackney and beyond, including free museum activities, outdoor adventures, and a chance to get creative. With a mix of indoor and outdoor fun, you can make the most of the lovely weather.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed and engaging environment for toddlers to explore books and stories. Free and local!

transport.fromHackney: Numerous buses serve Hackney Showroom. Check Citymapper for the best route.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to a local park for a full afternoon of fun.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Listen, play and make some noise in this informal session.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for the youngest visitors, allowing for sensory exploration and musical discovery. The museum itself is very toddler-friendly.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Arrive early as spaces are first-come, first-served.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life.
activities.whyItWorks: Beloved characters and a visually engaging exhibition will capture a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a free activity at the Young V&A for a full day out.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play space with story worlds and a story garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Two floors of immersive play space, plus a story garden, offer plenty of opportunities for active play and imaginative exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: Community farm with a variety of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see animals up close, in an accessible East London location. Always a hit with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for special events, especially around Easter.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.description: One of the largest city farms, with a wide variety of animals and free entry.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around, a variety of animals to see, and it's free! A great option for a low-cost day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Mudchute station.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with toddlers in mind, this playground provides a safe and engaging space for little ones to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the nearby Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.

activities.title: All Aboard play area
activities.description: All Aboard is a play area suitable for 0 – 7 with a fleet of mini vehicles of trains
activities.whyItWorks: The play area provides a number of opportunities for young kids to engage in fun and imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the overground line to Liverpool Street Station then take the central line to Holborn Station, then a short walk to the museum
transport.tip: Check their website for special events, especially around Easter.

activities.title: The Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and the wonders of the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Also, the museum offers family-friendly tours and activities, and it's free!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: The Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and space exploration.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light, and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is also great for sensory exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed ticket in advance.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood
activities.description: The Victoria & Albert Museum of Childhood is a small museum featuring toys and childhood artefacts from the 17th to the 21st century.
activities.whyItWorks: There are a few interactive exhibits including a sandpit, sensory playground and daily scheduled arts and crafts activities

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green Station, then a short walk
transport.tip: Check the V&A's website for all details about options and timings.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a variety of animals make this a great option for a budget-friendly day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Golders Green
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic city views.
activities.whyItWorks: Amazing views and greenery make this a sensory experience. It's also free!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Monument.
transport.tip: Book tickets well in advance.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: A unique urban playground and park for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Expansive outdoor space and playgrounds area that you can spend all day in, totally enclosed if case of runners!

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Russell Square Station
transport.tip: Check the website for special events

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Museum with natural history, musical instruments, and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal walk, gardens, and interactive exhibits make it fun for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check out Family Craft Saturdays!

activities.title: Family Craft Saturdays
activities.description: Family craft activities inspired by the Museum's collections.
activities.whyItWorks: The craft activities cater to children and enhance the value of the museum visit.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Children must be accompanied by an adult.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore installations to help the bees.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for Easter weekend.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: It is an outdoor energy-burner. Dress for wind. Bring snacks. Let them run.

transport.fromHackney: Trains run from Waterloo to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Go early in the day – the grounds are calmer before lunch.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids will search for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston, then a bus to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Book your zoo tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Cutty Sark
activities.price: Free with entry to the ship
activities.description: Follow the trail to spot hidden eggs around the ship. Collect the letters on each egg and unscramble the secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: The exploration of the ship with a fun activity is sure to be a blast.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid queues.

activities.title: Easter adventures with Bunny Ranger
activities.price: £3.50 per child, plus normal National Trust admission
activities.description: Prefect spring day out to enjoy nature and have fun.
activities.whyItWorks: A spring day out combined with an Easter activity is a good combination for outdoor entertainment.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Morden
transport.tip: National Trust members get free entry.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Join Alice and tumble down the rabbit hole into the fantastical world of Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: High energy performances with lots of visuals and music makes the show enjoyable for the whole family

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hammersmith Station
transport.tip: Book in advance!

activities.title: British Library's Fairy Tales exhibition
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: The magical displays are sure to be a treat for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Tube to British Library
transport.tip: Check the schedule for guided tours

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families includes super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: With a wide variety of options and shows there is something for everyone.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Southbank Centre
transport.tip: Check the schedule for free shows.
